description: prove the compute environment is hardened — pinned + hashed deps, deps.lock.json matches, CycloneDX SBOM emitted, wheelhouse manifest present, the compute Dockerfile tag/checksum-pinned, osv clean/waivered (P0-T14)
rules: every requirement is version== AND --hash= pinned (0 unpinned)
deps.lock.json is hash-pinned and its lock_sha256 matches the lock byte-for-byte (not stale)
a CycloneDX SBOM with components exists; the wheelhouse carries a SHA256SUMS manifest
the compute Dockerfile base is tag/digest-pinned (not :latest) and every fetched toolchain is SHA256-pinned
if osv-scanner is on PATH, the lock scans clean (else recorded as not-run)
usage: Set LOCK (requirements.lock) + optional DEPS_LOCK, SBOM, WHEELHOUSE (a dir with SHA256SUMS), DOCKERFILE. Output: harden.json. Exit 0 iff the environment is hardened.
limitation: Static artifact verification — it proves the pins/SBOM/Dockerfile are present and self-consistent, not that the built image's running versions match (that is the image build + the smoke test). osv depth depends on osv-scanner being installed.
